description: Dereferencing a user-supplied pointer directly in the kernel instead of using copy_from_user, allowing crashes or arbitrary-memory access.
body: Failure mode: a syscall or ioctl reads through a pointer passed from user space directly, so a malicious or buggy program can pass a kernel address or an invalid one; the kernel either faults (panic) or, worse, reads/writes memory it should not, becoming an exploit primitive. Detection signals: user pointer used without copy_from_user/get_user; oops on a crafted bad pointer; security scanner flags an unchecked __user pointer. Do not dereference user pointers directly. Safer fallback: copy data in with copy_from_user/get_user, check the return for faults, validate sizes and indices before use, and add a fault-injection test passing an invalid pointer that must return -EFAULT without crashing.
